data.title: Crypto execs flock to DC to support Senate stablecoin bill
data.description:
Crypto founders are heading to Washington, DC, to meet with lawmakers ahead of another expected vote on a stablecoin bill that initially failed in the Senate, according to Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong. In a May 14 X post from the US Capitol rotunda, Armstrong said as many as “60 [crypto] founders” had gathered in DC to support the Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for US Stablecoins, or GENIUS Act, being considered in the Senate and a draft of the market structure bill moving through the House of Representatives. The Coinbase CEO said the Senate could consider another vote on the GENIUS Act “hopefully tomorrow” after it failed to get enough support from Democrats on May 8. “Like any good negotiation, there’s a lot of details to work out at the last minute, but we’ve been stressing the urgency of this,” said Armstrong
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/crypto-companies-congress-crypto-bill?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: VanEck Introduces New ETF Focused on Blockchain Ecosystem
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, VanEck has unveiled an actively managed exchange-traded fund (ETF) aimed at investing in stocks and financial instruments that provide exposure to the digital economy. Announced on May 14, the VanEck Onchain Economy ETF (NODE) is now listed on the Cboe exchange. This fund seeks to offer investors comprehensive access to companies within the blockchain ecosystem, including crypto miners, exchanges, infrastructure providers, and financial technology platforms oriented towards cryptocurrency.

The NODE ETF will consider investing in any company that has clearly communicated its intentions to engage in the blockchain space, as demonstrated through public filings, earnings calls, or investor materials. While the fund will be actively managed, it will not hold cryptocurrencies directly but may invest in crypto-related financial instruments. Matthew Sigel, VanEck’s head of digital asset research and the portfolio manager for the NODE ETF, emphasized the fund's strategy to continuously update its investable universe as new companies emerge through IPOs, spinouts, or strategic shifts. Sigel also highlighted the importance of adjusting beta and volatility to maintain responsible exposure to bitcoin and businesses driving the growth of the onchain economy, ensuring that the fund avoids over-allocation to high-beta names during volatile markets and preserves buying power for future opportunities.

In April, VanEck launched another ETF, the VanEck Digital Transformation ETF (DAPP), which tracks a passive index of companies operating in the digital asset space. As of May 14, the DAPP ETF holds $185 million in net assets. Asset managers like VanEck are actively seeking approval from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to list approximately 70 crypto ETFs. This surge in ETF filings follows U.S. President Donald Trump’s decision to soften the SEC’s regulatory stance on cryptocurrencies after assuming office in January. On May 5, VanEck submitted a request to the SEC for approval to list an ETF that includes the BNB Chain’s native token.

data.title: Antalpha Debuts on Nasdaq with $49.3 Million IPO
data.description: According to Odaily, Antalpha, a crypto fintech company focused on serving Bitcoin mining enterprises, has announced its initial public offering (IPO) on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker symbol 'ANTA'. The company has priced its 3.85 million shares at $12.80 each. The IPO is expected to raise approximately $49.3 million, with the potential to reach up to $56.7 million if the over-allotment option is exercised. The funds raised will be allocated towards product development, global expansion, customer financing support, and investments in Bitcoin and digital gold.
